questions.question: What are the main components of RMI architecture and what role does each play?
expectedPoints: Stub: Client-side proxy that handles marshaling and communication
Skeleton: Server-side entity that receives requests and dispatches to actual object
Remote Interface: Defines methods that can be invoked remotely
RMI Registry: Naming service for locating remote objects
Remote Object: Actual implementation on the server side

questions.question: Compare and contrast RPC and RMI, discussing their paradigms, parameter passing mechanisms, and typical use cases.
expectedPoints: Paradigm: RPC is procedural, RMI is object-oriented
Parameter passing: RPC uses basic data types, RMI supports objects and remote references
Polymorphism: RMI supports it, RPC does not
State management: RMI can maintain object state, RPC typically stateless
Language support: RPC is cross-language, RMI is often language-specific
Use cases: RPC for simple cross-language communication, RMI for object-oriented distributed systems
